message:
linker hygiene: export BSD/glibc compat symbols and drop duplicate stubs from glibc_internal_abi

Two related changes that line up the libc.map version script with the
canonical implementations already living in dedicated modules.

`crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map` adds the following
public symbol exports under `GLIBC_2.2.5 { global: ... }` so callers
linking against `libc.so` can resolve them at runtime instead of
silently falling through to the host's libc:

- BSD sort family alongside `qsort`/`bsearch`: `mergesort`, `heapsort`,
  `radixsort`, `sradixsort` (implementations live in
  `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs`).
- BSD humanize / number-parsing / mode helpers next to `confstr` and
  `getbsize`: `strtonum`, `dehumanize_number`, `expand_number`,
  `humanize_number`, `setmode`, `getmode`, plus the BSD `pidfile_open`,
  `pidfile_write`, `pidfile_close`, `pidfile_remove`, `pidfile_fileno`
  family (also in `stdlib_abi.rs`).
- BSD stdio line helpers next to `fmemopen`/`open_memstream`/
  `fopencookie`: `fgetln`, `fparseln`
  (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs:7003,7112`).
- BSD `flopen`/`flopenat` next to `flock` in the file lifecycle group
  (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:18981`).

`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs` drops two stale
duplicate definitions that conflicted with the canonical exports:

- `__pipe(pipefd)`: removed from `glibc_internal_abi`. The canonical
  implementation lives at `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_abi.rs:119`
  and dispatches the real `sys_pipe2(pipefd, 0)` syscall; the stub
  here was identical and only existed because the symbol historically
  shipped from glibc's internal bucket.
- `sys_siglist`: removed (the leading-underscore `_sys_siglist` and
  `_sys_siglist`/`__sys_siglist` markers stay). The populated array
  is in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs` (covered by
  `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/string_abi_test.rs:2428` —
  `sys_siglist_has_65_entries`, `sys_siglist_well_known_descriptions`,
  etc.) and a second tentative-NULL definition here lets the linker
  bind callers to the wrong symbol.

Net effect: the version script now mirrors what we actually export,
and the internal compat module no longer shadows real implementations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
